title: Investigate why a change rebuilds so much
description: Use bazel query and the execution log to find the dependency edge that makes a small edit trigger a large rebuild.
body: Use when a localized change causes an unexpectedly large rebuild. Do not accept the cost without tracing it. Actions: identify the changed target; run bazel query rdeps to list reverse dependents; use somepath to find the edge linking them; inspect the execution log to confirm which actions re-ran. Validation signals include locating the over-broad dependency and a smaller rebuild after splitting it. Risk boundary: refactoring the graph can break distant consumers, so confirm reverse dependents with rdeps before changing edges.
